2019 SUBARU OUTBACK. CONSUMER WRITES IN REGARD TO DESIGN OR MALFUNCTIONING OF TECHNOLOGY. THE ADAPTIVE CRUISE CONTROL FAILED TO MAINTAIN ADJUSTED SPEED. *AS     THE CONSUMER STATED WHILE DRIVING THE CRUISE CONTROL WAS SET TO 45MPH AT WHICH POINT, THE VEHICLE SPEED UP AND CRASHED

narrative.headline: 2019 Subaru Outback: electrical and visibility issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: The most commonly reported problem involves the electrical system, where owners describe the infotainment and navigation system freezing entirely and taking the backup camera and radio down with it. A large share of reports also concerns visibility and wipers, with many of those complaints similarly tied to the frozen infotainment display losing the backup camera feed. Windshield cracking and reduced engine braking behavior round out the remaining owner concerns.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the navigation system freezing completely from early ownership, causing the radio, backup camera, and other connected functions to stop working simultaneously. · 1 crash

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Owners report loss of the backup camera display, described in the context of the infotainment system freezing and taking visibility-related functions offline.

issues.title: Visibility
issues.blurb: Owners report small cracks appearing in the middle of the front windshield that expand further in hot weather conditions.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report that the engine does not provide braking force when traveling downhill, which they describe as a departure from the behavior of prior model generations of the same vehicle. · 1 fire · 4 crash · 1 injured

issues.sample: 2019 SUBARU OUTBACK. CONSUMER WRITES IN REGARD TO DESIGN OR MALFUNCTIONING OF TECHNOLOGY. THE ADAPTIVE CRUISE CONTROL FAILED TO MAINTAIN ADJUSTED SPEED. *AS     THE CONSUMER STATED WHILE DRIVING THE CRUISE CONTROL WAS SET TO 45MPH AT WHICH POINT, THE VEHICLE SPEED UP AND CRASHED
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The most safety-relevant category: only 36 reports (4%) but 8 crashes and 6 injuries. Test the driver-assist features carefully.
